goal: Move sparse-MLA backward toward a HIP/CK/rocWMMA cooperative producer-consumer kernel where the score/prob/dP tile is consumed before any HBM score slab.

rocwmma_extension_attempt.symptom: rocWMMA include path pulled both hip/amd_detail/amd_warp_sync_functions.h and hip/amd_detail/amd_warp_sync_functions_hip.h, causing duplicate definitions such as __hip_readfirstlane, __syncwarp, __ballot_sync, and shuffle/reduce helpers.
rocwmma_extension_attempt.mitigation_tried: Split PyTorch/PyBind C++ from the HIP translation unit and retried with -UHIP_ENABLE_WARP_SYNC_BUILTINS.

standalone_hipcc_check.note: Compiling the PyTorch-preprocessed kernel_hip.hip failed because it includes hip/hip_runtime_hip.h, but compiling the raw kernel.hip directly succeeds.

rocwmma_extension_attempt.read: The previous Torch/Thrust all-in-one failure is gone, and raw standalone hipcc can compile the rocWMMA source. The blocker is PyTorch's JIT extension hipify/injected-flag path, so the next rocWMMA/CK implementation should use standalone HIP object packaging, CK, or a custom build carrier instead of torch.utils.cpp_extension load(..., cuda_sources=[rocwmma_source]) directly.

contract.formula: dKV[g,d] += sum_{token,k,h:group(token,k)=g} prob[token,h,k] * dO[token,h,d] + scaled_dP[token,h,k] * q[token,h,d]

prebuilt_object_carrier_followup.patch: tools/dsv4_runtime_probe/probe_sparse_mla_source_boundary_hip_coop.py now supports --hip-build-mode prebuilt_object, which compiles raw HIP with hipcc -fPIC and links the object into the Torch C++ wrapper.

pure_hip_scalar_prebuilt.read: Confirms the raw HIP object carrier works. The scalar D16 replay body remains deeply rejected.

rocwmma_source_boundary_prebuilt.read: Bypassing PyTorch hipify lets rocWMMA source compile, link, and run. The schedule is still rejected because it recomputes QK, dO.V, probability, and scaled-dP for every D16 tile before the rocWMMA contribution step.

prebuilt_object_carrier_followup.build_read: The build blocker is solved for future rocWMMA/CK experiments: use raw hipcc object packaging or CK, not torch.utils.cpp_extension cuda_sources hipify, when the HIP source includes rocWMMA.

score_reuse_d64_followup.read: Computing score/prob/scaled-dP once for a wider D64 CTA cuts scalar replay versus D16, but it is still seconds-scale. This rejects scalar score reuse as the main route and keeps the target on an MFMA/CK-style producer-consumer score page with group-aware epilogue.

rocwmma_score_page_d512_followup.read: This is the first source-boundary rung that actually uses a workgroup-local score/prob/scaled-dP page and rocWMMA producer-consumer math. It proves the mechanism by cutting the scalar D64 path from 2042 ms to 105 ms, but it is still not a promoted sparse-attention backward candidate: it only returns dKV, uses global atomics, and remains about 9.4x slower than the current Triton full sparse-attention boundary.

rocwmma_score_page_d512_decomposition.read: The 105 ms wall is not only the final dKV atomic/write path. The score-page producer alone is already 32.57 ms and the contribution sweep adds 29.75 ms before dKV atomics add 43.11 ms. Therefore the next branch should be a CK/MFMA production schedule that improves the score producer and contribution sweep, while also replacing the global-atomic dKV epilogue.

rejected_simple_tiles.read: Slightly loses to 16x64 despite more CTAs.
More CTAs but lower selected-panel coalescing.
Too little in-tile route reduction and too many small panels.

rocwmma_score_page_d512_tile_sweep.read: The original 32x64 CTA serialized too much work. Simple tiling can cut the bring-up kernel to 66.47 ms, but that is still about 6x slower than the current Triton full sparse-attention backward gate. This keeps the path as a design probe, not a promoted latency candidate.

rocwmma_score_page_d512_reusekv.mechanism: Flip score-page production from h0->d0 to d0->h0 so one selected-KV D16 tile is reused across all four H16 head blocks before advancing D.
rocwmma_score_page_d512_reusekv.read: The KV-D reuse variant is the best current rocWMMA bring-up kernel at 59.25 ms. It also shifts the attribution: final dKV global atomic/write delta is now about 7.42 ms, while score+contribution is still 51.83 ms. That makes producer/contribution scheduling the next dominant target.

rocwmma_score_page_d512_multiwave.mechanism: Keep the D512 score page and KV-D reuse, but use a 256-thread CTA with four wavefronts: one wavefront per H16 head block for score-page production and one wavefront per D16 contribution consumer for four consecutive D blocks.
rocwmma_score_page_d512_multiwave.read: The four-wave variant validates the same-workgroup score-page producer/consumer mechanism and is the best current rocWMMA bring-up at 43.82 ms, but it is still dKV-only and about 3.94x slower than the 11.13 ms full Triton sparse-attention backward boundary. Do not run e2e TFLOPs from this variant; use it as evidence for a lower-level CK/HIP/MFMA cooperative rewrite.

decision.rejected: Scalar pure-HIP D16 replay and D64 score-reuse source-boundary schedules.
decision.retained: Source-boundary no-HBM-slab contract, split-source HIP extension build surface, and the same-workgroup D512 score-page producer-consumer mechanism as a bring-up rung. The best bring-up point is now 16x64 with SCORE_PAGE_REUSE_KV_D=1, SCORE_PAGE_MULTI_WAVE=1, and SCORE_PAGE_THREADS=256 at 43.82 ms.
decision.next_kernel_shape: Keep the D512 score page idea, the 16x64 simple-tiling lesson, KV-D reuse, and the four-wave same-workgroup producer/consumer lesson, but stop extending this rocWMMA dKV-only emitter. Build a production full-boundary cooperative kernel that returns dQ+dKV, uses CK/custom-HIP MFMA for QK, dO.V, and contribution math with better occupancy/reuse than the current rocWMMA bring-up, and adds a grouped/segmented dKV epilogue before global writes.
implementation_options: CK tile or custom HIP kernel with explicit MFMA fragments and a shared score/prob/dP page.
Standalone rocWMMA/hipcc build outside the current PyTorch extension header path, if we still want rocWMMA fragments.
Persistent workgroup design that owns token tile plus selected tile and fans a small score page to D-block consumers without an HBM score slab.
